meaning: n.窗帘,帷幕,面纱,(妇女闭门不出的)身闺制度
examples: He kept his daughters in virtual purdah.
The purdah of the Athens Olympic Games has fallen.
Separately, you will notice a rash of transport announcements over the coming weeks as TfL's mayoral election purdah starts on 20 March.
Her autobiography " From Purdah to Parliament" is a mesmerizing tale of a fearless Muslim woman who fought her way from veiled to valiant.
